channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Economic Malaise and Political Inaction: A Nation in Stagnation
item.description: The article highlights the growing economic challenges facing Australia, with the past 10 weeks revealing a nation struggling with stagnation and declining productivity. Politicians are urged to addre...

item.title: Andy Burnham's Leadership Transition and Policy Commitments
item.description: The article details Andy Burnham's ascension as the new leader of the Labour Party, emphasizing his pledge to prioritize 'unashamedly Labour' policies. It highlights his plans for the party's future, ...

item.title: Unifying the Sea Peoples: Tracing the Bronze Age collapse through ancient texts and archaeology
item.description: The article explores the enigmatic 'Sea Peoples' mentioned in ancient texts and their potential role in the collapse of the Bronze Age world. While often blamed for the downfall of civilizations like ...

item.title: Fourth Suspect in American Scientist's Killing Surrenders
item.description: A fourth suspect in the murder case of an American scientist has surrendered to authorities, according to a report by ABS-CBN News. The incident, which occurred in Negros Oriental, has drawn significa...

item.title: Missing Calgary Boy Last Seen at Cambrian Heights Day Home: Police Search Ongoing
item.description: A 12-year-old boy from Calgary has been reported missing after last being seen at a day home in the Cambrian Heights neighborhood on July 16, 2026. Police have launched an investigation to locate the ...

item.title: Mother Arranges Introduction Without Warning Sparks Social Media Reaction
item.description: A Gen Z woman received a WhatsApp message from an unknown man who claimed her mother had given him her number while riding a bus. The stranger's reply, revealing the source of her contact information,...

item.title: Adaptation Over Strength: Lessons from Zinj Day
item.description: The article discusses the significance of Zinj Day, commemorating the 1959 discovery of Zinjanthropus boisei fossils at Olduvai Gorge. It highlights how physical strength alone did not ensure survival...

item.title: Exploring Japanese Retro-Futurism of the 1960s: Nostalgia Meets Visionary Design
item.description: The article delves into the unique allure of Japanese retro-futurism from the 1960s, highlighting how it merges nostalgia with visionary design elements distinct from Western counterparts. Unlike West...

item.title: Kaduna State Launches Comprehensive Health Initiatives to Combat Malnutrition
item.description: The Kaduna State government has pledged to strengthen its efforts in combating malnutrition through targeted health interventions. Officials highlighted key metrics such as Vitamin A supplementation c...

item.title: Japan Adjusts Imperial Rules, Yet Women Remain Excluded from Throne
item.description: Japan's imperial rules have undergone minor adjustments, allowing female members of the royal family to potentially ascend the throne under specific conditions. However, the core principle of male-onl...

item.title: DCP Wins Ol Kalou By-Election, Defying UDA's Expectations
item.description: The Democracy for the Citizens Party (DCP) secured a landslide victory in the Ol Kalou by-election, defeating the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) candidate. The by-election followed the death of MP D...

item.title: Historical Events of July 17: Moments That Shaped Nations and Changed the World
item.description: The article commemorates July 17 across history, highlighting pivotal events that transformed global narratives. Key moments include the end of the Hundred Years War in 1453, the 1836 wreck of the shi...

item.title: Two Deaths Reported During Odisha's Puri Rath Yatra Amid Government Denial of Stampede
item.description: During the annual Puri Rath Yatra in Odisha, two individuals lost their lives due to an 'unprecedented rush' of devotees, according to reports by The Indian Express. The state government has refuted c...

item.title: Protesters Block Ulega Malinyi's Convoy, Highlighting Political Tensions
item.description: A convoy carrying prominent Tanzanian politician Ulega Malinyi was blocked by traditional drums and barricades, reflecting rising political tensions ahead of upcoming elections. The incident highlight...

item.title: Joint operation results in killing of five suspected terrorists in Bannu district
item.description: In a coordinated joint operation between the Counter-Terrorism Department (CTD) and local police forces, five suspected terrorists were killed in the Bannu district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa. The operatio...

item.title: Spain and Argentina Clash in World Cup Final: Tactical Mastery and Messi's Leadership
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup final promises a clash between two elite teams: Spain, the reigning European champions, and Argentina, the defending World Cup holders. Both teams have reached the final unbeat...

item.title: Canadian Institute for Military and Veteran Health Research Supports Military Families
item.description: The article highlights the work of the Canadian Institute for Military and Veteran Health Research (CIMVHR), which focuses on improving the health and well-being of military personnel, veterans, and t...

item.title: Melissa Gilbert faces backlash after being axed from her TV series amid legal issues, following her husband's claims of cancellation over sexual abuse allegations
item.description: The article discusses the controversy surrounding Melissa Gilbert, an actress known for her role in 'The West Wing,' who was recently removed from a hit show following allegations of child sex charges...

item.title: Philippine Coast Guard Spokesperson Calls for China's Apology Over Racist Video Depicting Filipinos as Monkeys
item.description: Philippine Coast Guard spokesperson Rear Adm. Jay Tarriela challenged senators Alan Peter Cayetano and Robin Padilla to demand an apology from China over a state-run media video that depicted Filipino...

item.title: Actors Motlatsi Mafatshe and Mapaseka Koetle Discuss Marriage, Betrayal, and Second Chances in New Telenovela 'Nyala O'
item.description: The article highlights the new Mzansi Magic telenovela 'Nyala O', starring Motlatsi Mafatshe as Rhami and Mapaseka Koetle as Kea. The show explores complex themes of marriage, infidelity, financial st...
